---
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```



```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(irr)      # for icc

# Read the data
LLM_a <- read_xlsx("AD2_en_annotated_a_20260108.xlsx") # prompt a
LLM_b <- read_xlsx("AD2_en_annotated_b_20260108.xlsx") # prompt b
LLM_c <- read_xlsx("AD2_en_annotated_c_20260108.xlsx") # prompt c

```


```{r}
# Function to calculate all metrics for a given dataframe
calculate_metrics <- function(df, df_name) {
  # Calculate correlation
  cor_value <- cor(df$AD_total, df$gpt4.1_AD_total)
  
  # Calculate Mean Absolute Error
  mae_value <- mean(abs(df$AD_total - df$gpt4.1_AD_total))
  
  # Prepare data for ICC (two-column matrix)
  icc_data <- cbind(df$AD_total, df$gpt4.1_AD_total)
  
  # Calculate ICC (two-way, absolute agreement)
  icc_result <- icc(icc_data, model = "oneway", type = "agreement")
  
  # Print results
  cat("\n=== Metrics for", df_name, "===\n")
  cat("Correlation:", round(cor_value, 2), "\n")
  cat("Mean Absolute Error:", round(mae_value, 2), "\n")
  cat("ICC (one-way, absolute agreement):", round(icc_result$value, 2), "\n")
  cat("ICC 95% CI: [", round(icc_result$lbound, 2), ", ", 
      round(icc_result$ubound, 2), "]\n", sep = "")
  
  # Return results as a list
  return(list(
    correlation = cor_value,
    mae = mae_value,
    icc = icc_result$value,
    icc_ci = c(icc_result$lbound, icc_result$ubound)
  ))
}

# Calculate metrics for each dataframe
results_a <- calculate_metrics(LLM_a, "LLM_a")
results_b <- calculate_metrics(LLM_b, "LLM_b")
results_c <- calculate_metrics(LLM_c, "LLM_c")

# Alternative: Store all results in a summary dataframe
summary_results <- tibble(
  Dataset = c("LLM_a", "LLM_b", "LLM_c"),
  Correlation = c(
    round(cor(LLM_a$AD_total, LLM_a$gpt4.1_AD_total), 2),
    round(cor(LLM_b$AD_total, LLM_b$gpt4.1_AD_total), 2),
    round(cor(LLM_c$AD_total, LLM_c$gpt4.1_AD_total), 2)
  ),
  MAE = c(
    round(mean(abs(LLM_a$AD_total - LLM_a$gpt4.1_AD_total)), 2),
    round(mean(abs(LLM_b$AD_total - LLM_b$gpt4.1_AD_total)), 2),
    round(mean(abs(LLM_c$AD_total - LLM_c$gpt4.1_AD_total)), 2)
  )
)

# For ICC in summary, you can use the results from the function above
summary_results$ICC <- c(
  round(results_a$icc, 2),
  round(results_b$icc, 2),
  round(results_c$icc, 2)
)

# Print the summary table
cat("\n=== Summary Table ===\n")
print(summary_results)

# If you want the original correlations too:
cat("\n=== Original Correlations ===\n")
cat("LLM_a:", round(cor(LLM_a$AD_total, LLM_a$gpt4.1_AD_total), 2), "\n")
cat("LLM_b:", round(cor(LLM_b$AD_total, LLM_b$gpt4.1_AD_total), 2), "\n")
cat("LLM_c:", round(cor(LLM_c$AD_total, LLM_c$gpt4.1_AD_total), 2), "\n")


```
